title: Alsine calycantha
taxonomy: Alsine calycantha is classified within the family Caryophyllaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the pink or carnation family. It is further classified as a species within the genus Alsine. Alsine calycantha is native to the mountainous regions of Europe, where it grows in rocky areas and meadows. The plant is a low-growing, herbaceous perennial with small white or pink flowers. Alsine calycantha is also known by other names, such as mountain sorrel or Alsine.

intro: Alsine calycantha, also known as Alpine Pennycress or Mountain Penny-cress, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Brassicaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as arthritis, skin conditions, and respiratory problems.
